_comment: WRG-11 packages the version sentry monitors. Each entry has a GitHub repo slug, plus optionally a PyPI name for PyPI-published packages. GitHub-only distributions (Claude Code plugins, MCP servers shipped via plugin marketplace) have pypi_name=null and skip the PyPI half of the drift check -- the dashboard tolerates the null and renders a '?' for the PyPI column. The sentry compares PyPI .info.version against the latest GitHub Release tag (with optional leading 'v' stripped). Drift means one channel was bumped without the other.

targets.name: instinct
targets._note: PyPI dist name (instinct-mcp) differs from GitHub repo name (instinct) -- the plain 'instinct' name was already taken on PyPI when v1.0 shipped, so the package publishes under instinct-mcp. Config drift caught by sentry's 1st commissioning run (DRIFT detected against the wrong package); SB-16 1st canonical 'sentry config drift -- PyPI name != GH name'.

targets.name: wrg-sigma-rules
targets._note: GitHub-only distribution -- Claude Code plugin (Anthropic CC marketplace + Glama MCP catalog) + standalone MCP server. No PyPI package; the 3 MCP tools are imported directly from the cloned repo. Multi-channel ship 2026-05-26: Glama v1.0.0 LIVE, Anthropic CC marketplace pending listing. 1st GH-only target in the portfolio; exercises the pypi_name=null code path.
